title: isotrema grande
parts: Isotrema grande has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, roots, and bark, which are used to treat various ailments. The leaves of the Isotrema grande plant are rich in flavonoids, including kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of the plant contain compounds like saponins and glycosides, which are used to treat fever and digestive issues. The bark of Isotrema grande is a source of tannins and phenolic acids, which have antimicrobial and antifungal properties.
side_effects: Isotrema grande can have side effects if used improperly, such as skin irritation, including redness, itching, and dryness, as well as more severe reactions like severe acne, skin infections, and even scarring. In some cases, long-term use can lead to more serious health issues, including increased risk of depression, anxiety, and even suicidal thoughts. Additionally, improper use of isotrema grande may also cause gastrointestinal problems, such as nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, as well as liver damage and increased risk of certain cancers. It is essential to consult a medical professional before using isotrema grande to minimize the risk of these side effects and ensure safe and effective treatment.
